  3. Karlsruhe Palace

Landmark · City Center West

Karlsruhe Palace

Karlsruhe Palace was built from 1715 by Margrave Karl Wilhelm as his residence and the geometric center of the new fan-shaped city. From the 42-meter-high palace tower, 32 streets and avenues radiate outward to the south. Today the palace houses the Baden State Museum with extensive archaeological and cultural-history collections.

Free entry Tue–Sun 10am–6pm LandmarksHistoryMuseums
Official website →

In the district

City Center West

Schlossplatz, market square and the western pedestrian zone. Karlsruhe's baroque heart and the origin of the fan layout.

View district hub →

More attractions in the district

Park
Free

City Center West

Palace Gardens

English landscape park behind the palace with a botanical garden and tree stands more than a hundred years old.

Free entry
Museum

City Center West

State Art Gallery Karlsruhe

One of the oldest and most important picture galleries in Germany. Cranach, Grünewald, Rembrandt and Caspar David Friedrich.

from 8 EUR
Market Square
Free

City Center West

Market Square with the Pyramid

A reddish sandstone pyramid serving as the tomb of city founder Karl Wilhelm. The neoclassical heart of the city.

Free entry
Church
Free

City Center West

St Stephen's Church

A neoclassical rotunda by Friedrich Weinbrenner, modeled on the Pantheon in Rome.

Free entry

Karlsruhe interactive. Fan-shaped city, KIT, ZKM and the Federal Constitutional Court. Attractions, hotels, districts and tech news on one portal.
